eligibility_criteria.rationale: Must reforest lands that historically supported forest ecosystems, with robust evidence
eligibility_criteria.plain_english: The land must have been a forest in the past, proven through data, imagery, or scientific analysis

eligibility_criteria.rationale: Must not reforest lands where deforestation occurred within 10 years prior to start (exceptions for natural disasters/common practice)
eligibility_criteria.plain_english: The land cannot have been deliberately cleared in the last decade before planting begins

eligibility_criteria.rationale: Must not occur on terrestrial or tidal wetlands (peatlands, marshes, mangroves)
eligibility_criteria.plain_english: Cannot plant on water-logged soils or swampy areas

eligibility_criteria.rationale: Must not be in regions where albedo changes cause net warming (Hasler et al. 2024 data)
eligibility_criteria.plain_english: Tree cover cannot make local climate warmer by changing how sunlight reflects off the land

eligibility_criteria.rationale: Must prove activities wouldn't occur without carbon finance via dynamic baselining
eligibility_criteria.plain_english: Must show trees wouldn't have grown naturally or through normal practices without carbon credits

eligibility_criteria.rationale: Must be degraded lands holding less than expected mature biomass OR not meeting forest definition
eligibility_criteria.plain_english: Land must currently be in poor condition compared to what a healthy forest would be

errors_in_original_list.note: This criterion does not appear in v1.1 protocol
This criterion is not explicitly stated in v1.1
Must be degraded/non-forested NOW but must have been historically forested

metadata.notes: This analysis is based on the publicly available v1.1 protocol document. Some page numbers are listed as 'varies' because the web version does not provide explicit page numbers for all sections.
